articles.title: Grammarly’s sloppelganger saga
articles.summary: Grammarly's AI writing assistant faces scrutiny over quality and ethical concerns. The article examines challenges with AI-generated suggestions and broader implications for AI reliability.

articles.title: A folk musician became a target for AI fakes and a copyright troll
articles.summary: Folk musician Murphy Campbell discovered AI-generated covers of her songs uploaded to Spotify without her permission. The incident highlights vulnerabilities in copyright systems and streaming platforms' protection against AI-created deepfakes.

articles.title: Really, you made this without AI? Prove it
articles.summary: As AI-generated content becomes harder to distinguish from human work, there's growing skepticism about platforms' failure to label AI content, prompting a potential need for human-made content certification.

articles.title: Anthropic says Claude Code subscribers will need to pay extra for OpenClaw usage
articles.summary: Anthropic will charge Claude Code subscribers additional fees for using OpenClaw and third-party tools with their coding assistant, increasing overall costs.

articles.title: Anthropic is having a moment in the private markets; SpaceX could spoil the party
articles.summary: Anthropic dominates private share trading while OpenAI loses momentum. SpaceX's upcoming IPO could reshape the private markets landscape.

articles.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
articles.summary: OpenAI COO Brad Lightcap transitions to lead special projects. CMO Kate Rouch steps back for cancer recovery with plans to return.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway secured $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native cloud infrastructure, challenging AWS with a platform that has attracted 2 million developers. The investment capitalizes on developer frustration with legacy cloud platforms' complexity and cost.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code costs up to $200/month for AI-powered coding, but Goose offers identical functionality for free as an open-source alternative that runs locally without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge. The creative recruitment stunt helped the startup attract top engineering talent in a competitive market.

articles.title: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
articles.summary: OpenClaw, a popular AI agentic tool with 347,000 GitHub stars, poses security risks by requiring broad system access to control computers and interact with apps. A recently patched vulnerability highlights dangers of trusting such tools with extensive permissions.

articles.title: New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs
articles.summary: Researchers discovered three new Rowhammer attacks exploiting Nvidia GPU memory vulnerabilities to gain complete root control of shared cloud machines. The attacks manipulate bit flips in DRAM through rapid memory access patterns.

articles.title: Quantum computers need vastly fewer resources than thought to break vital encryption
articles.summary: New research shows quantum computers could break elliptic curve encryption with far fewer resources than previously thought, potentially threatening current cryptographic security in under 10 minutes.

articles.title: Four things we’d need to put data centers in space
articles.summary: SpaceX filed to launch up to one million orbital data centers to power AI without environmental impact on Earth. Tech companies like Amazon and Google are exploring space-based computing infrastructure.

articles.title: The Download: plastic’s problem with fuel prices, and SpaceX’s blockbuster IPO
articles.summary: Rising fuel prices from geopolitical conflict are creating supply chain impacts that could increase plastic costs globally. Petrochemical-dependent plastic production faces economic pressures affecting American consumers.

articles.title: Fuel prices are soaring. Plastic could be next.
articles.summary: Rising fuel prices from Middle East tensions are now threatening plastic supply chains, as plastics depend on petrochemicals. This could have broader economic impacts beyond gasoline costs.

articles.title: Welcome Gemma 4: Frontier multimodal intelligence on device
articles.summary: Google announces Gemma 4, a new frontier multimodal AI model designed to run efficiently on-device, enabling advanced intelligence capabilities directly on user devices without cloud dependency.

articles.title: Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier
articles.summary: Holo3 represents a breakthrough in computer use AI, advancing how artificial intelligence interacts with digital systems. This development pushes boundaries in AI capabilities and automation.

articles.title: Falcon Perception
articles.summary: The article discusses Falcon Perception, likely a new AI perception or vision system. Details about its capabilities and applications are not provided in the content.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses future directions in AI development and innovation. The conversation explores emerging trends and next steps in artificial intelligence technology.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content handling and marketing operations.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural technology tools available to the public for sustainable farming innovation and development.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces two new Gemini API inference tiers: Flex for cost efficiency and Priority for lower latency, helping developers balance performance and expenses.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids now offers free AI-powered video creation and editing using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1 technology, enabling high-quality video generation without cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map aimed at protecting Brazil's forests from deforestation and environmental damage.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open models yet, designed specifically for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live introduces an improved voice model with better precision and reduced latency for more natural, fluid audio interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches AI's potential for harmful manipulation in finance and health sectors, developing new safety measures to protect people.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and claimed token-efficiency gains. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, an AI-powered graphics technology that combines 3D rendering with generative AI to enhance photorealism in real-time gaming up to 4K resolution.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR pricing, Cursor's coding automations, and new interactive visualizations from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: KiloClaw targets shadow AI with autonomous agent governance
articles.summary: KiloClaw addresses shadow AI by providing enterprises governance over autonomous agents deployed by employees outside official channels. The platform tackles security risks from unregulated agent deployments exposing proprietary data.

articles.title: 5 best practices to secure AI systems
articles.summary: AI systems require multi-layered security strategies including data protection, access control, and monitoring. Key practices include enforcing strict access governance, encryption, and role-based permissions to protect critical operations.

articles.title: China’s Five-Year Plan details the targets for AI deployment
articles.summary: China's 15th Five-Year Plan prioritizes AI development through 2030, focusing on high-performance chips, software, model architectures, and core algorithms. The plan integrates AI with quantum computing and biotechnology as strategic priorities, supported by advanced communications infrastructure.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, improving collaborative problem-solving debugging.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses key challenges in complex indoor environments by handling localization, self-positioning, and path planning.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, capitalizing on the OpenClaw AI agent trend.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by successfully balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, overcoming a historical challenge in AI models.

articles.title: Multi-level AI prompt engineering: A new tool for scientific discovery
articles.summary: Multi-level prompt engineering and parabolic extrapolation enabled an LLM to function as a theoretical collaborator, producing a testable multiverse model for scientific discovery.

articles.title: Meet ‘AutoAgent’: The Open-Source Library That Lets an AI Engineer and Optimize Its Own Agent Harness Overnight
articles.summary: AutoAgent is an open-source library that automatically optimizes AI agents without manual prompt-tuning. In 24-hour runs, it achieved top scores on SpreadsheetBench (96.5%) and TerminalBench (55.1%).

articles.title: Inside the Creative Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stack: Where Human Vision and Artificial Intelligence Meet to Design Future Fashion
articles.summary: AI transforms fashion design through algorithms and neural networks, replacing traditional intuition with machine learning. This technology now centrally shapes how clothes are designed and manufactured.

articles.title: Netflix AI Team Just Open-Sourced VOID: an AI Model That Erases Objects From Videos — Physics and All
articles.summary: Netflix's VOID model automatically removes objects from videos while preserving physics and interactions, eliminating tedious manual VFX work that traditionally takes weeks to make scenes look natural.

articles.title: I customized an Arch-based distro my way in under 5 minutes - and it's glorious
articles.summary: Prism Linux installer enables rapid customization of Arch-based distros in minutes, allowing users to curate software and achieve personalized out-of-the-box experiences effortlessly.

articles.title: How I beat the $4 gas average in 2026: These 5 apps show you the cheapest station nearby
articles.summary: Apps help drivers find the cheapest gas stations nearby as prices continue rising. Five applications use location data to identify lowest fuel costs in real-time.

articles.title: I let a smart planter maintain itself while I was away for 2 months - here's the result
articles.summary: A LeafyPod smart planter successfully maintained plants autonomously for 2 months while the owner was away, demonstrating its ability to help even inexperienced plant owners keep plants healthy.

articles.title: 7 Machine Learning Trends to Watch in 2026
articles.summary: Machine learning systems are evolving beyond backend dashboards. The article explores seven emerging trends expected to shape ML development in 2026.

articles.title: Building a ‘Human-in-the-Loop’ Approval Gate for Autonomous Agents
articles.summary: Agentic AI systems can implement state-managed interruptions to halt execution pipelines, creating human-in-the-loop approval gates for autonomous agents.

articles.title: From Prompt to Prediction: Understanding Prefill, Decode, and the KV Cache in LLMs
articles.summary: Article explores LLM inference mechanisms: prefill attention, decode phase, and KV cache optimization for efficient prediction generation from prompts.

articles.title: Building a Python Workflow That Catches Bugs Before Production
articles.summary: Modern tooling helps identify software defects earlier in the development lifecycle. This Python workflow catches bugs before production deployment.

articles.title: Building Robust Credit Scoring Models with Python
articles.summary: A practical guide for building robust credit scoring models in Python, focusing on measuring variable relationships for effective feature selection.

articles.title: DenseNet Paper Walkthrough: All Connected
articles.summary: DenseNet addresses the vanishing gradient problem in deep neural networks by improving weight updates during training. The architecture enables better gradient flow, allowing deeper networks to train more effectively.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: National Robotics Week highlights AI breakthroughs enabling robots to transform industries. Advancements in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models accelerate real-world deployment.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA collaborated to optimize Gemma 4, a family of small, fast models designed for efficient local AI execution across devices from RTX PCs to data centers, enabling on-device agentic AI with real-time context.

articles.title: Press Start on April: GeForce NOW Brings 10 Games to the Cloud
articles.summary: GeForce NOW launches 10 new games in April, including Capcom's PRAGMATA and Arknights: Endfield. The cloud gaming service expands its library with fresh titles for streaming.

articles.title: Simulate realistic users to evaluate multi-turn AI agents in Strands Evals
articles.summary: Strands Evals enables evaluation of multi-turn AI agents by simulating realistic user interactions beyond single-turn patterns, using systematic assessment frameworks for helpfulness, faithfulness, and tool usage.

articles.title: Scaling seismic foundation models on AWS: Distributed training with Amazon SageMaker HyperPod and expanding context windows
articles.summary: TGS optimized seismic foundation models using AWS SageMaker HyperPod, achieving near-linear scaling for distributed training and expanding context windows. Training time reduced from 6 months to 5 days.

articles.title: Control which domains your AI agents can access
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore enables controlled AI agent web access through AWS Network Firewall domain filtering in VPCs, preventing unauthorized data exfiltration and malware exposure while maintaining security compliance.

articles.title: Accelerating Vision AI Pipelines with Batch Mode VC-6 and NVIDIA Nsight
articles.summary: NVIDIA's batch mode VC-6 and Nsight tools accelerate vision AI pipelines by optimizing decode, preprocessing, and GPU scheduling to match improved model throughput.

articles.title: Bringing AI Closer to the Edge and On-Device with Gemma 4
articles.summary: Gemma 4 multimodal and multilingual models expand the Gemmaverse, scaling from NVIDIA Blackwell data centers to Jetson edge devices for diverse deployment needs.

articles.title: Achieving Single-Digit Microsecond Latency Inference for Capital Markets
articles.summary: AI inference latency has been reduced to single-digit microseconds for capital markets trading, enabling faster responses to market events using specialized hardware and neural networks.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta committed to achieving net zero emissions by 2030 and is developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint while making solutions publicly available.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with unconventional background developed innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive technological breakthroughs in emerging XR fields.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers present methods for identifying feature interactions in Large Language Models at scale to improve interpretability and transparency. This work aims to make LLM decision-making more understandable for building safer and more trustworthy AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research presents an information-driven approach to designing imaging systems that optimizes how optical systems map objects to images while managing noise. The method uses noisy measurements and noise models to evaluate measurement quality.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new reinforcement learning algorithm based on divide-and-conquer paradigm avoids temporal difference learning limitations, offering better scalability for long-horizon tasks in off-policy RL settings.
